title: The Client

jokes.text: Hey, Smith. / Yo, man, what's up? Come here. / What's up? What does this contract say? - 'Fresh Prince of Bel-Air.' - That's right, not Philadelphia, Bel-Air. - Yeah, man, but, you know, my mama- - Get in the van.
jokes.setup: Will narrates leaving family to work at cheesesteaks in Philly, establishing normal reality
jokes.punchline: NBC executive appears to force him back to the show via his contract
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke breaking the fourth wall to address Will Smith potentially leaving the show. Self-aware about TV contract obligations while maintaining the show's reality.

jokes.text: If you need me, I'll be hiding the silver.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey announces Jazz is here to see Will
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with his intention to hide valuables
jokes.explanation: Perfect Geoffrey character moment - his dry, proper delivery of class-based prejudice against Jazz. Economical and character-specific.

jokes.text: I tried to get in touch. Where are you staying? On the street. At least until Jewel lets me back in the house.
jokes.setup: Will asks where Jazz is staying, expecting normal answer
jokes.punchline: Jazz reveals he's homeless and locked out by his girlfriend
jokes.explanation: Typical Jazz loser humor - reveals his pathetic living situation matter-of-factly. Character-consistent but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: By the way, who's playing the mother this year?
jokes.setup: Jazz addressing the family after being away
jokes.punchline: References the real-life recasting of Aunt Viv between seasons
jokes.explanation: Bold meta-joke addressing the elephant in the room about recasting Aunt Viv. Takes balls to acknowledge this directly on the show.

jokes.text: It's the same mom. Who are you?
jokes.setup: Jazz asks about the mother recasting
jokes.punchline: Will denies it while new Aunt Viv asks who Jazz is
jokes.explanation: Perfect follow-up to the meta-joke - Will's denial while the new actress immediately proves Jazz's point by not recognizing him.

jokes.text: Man, I'm going back on the street where things make sense.
jokes.setup: Jazz witnesses the surreal meta-humor about recasting
jokes.punchline: Declares the street more logical than this TV reality
jokes.explanation: Good capper to the meta-sequence - Jazz's bewildered reaction sells the absurdity while staying true to his character.

jokes.text: Hey, Uncle Phil, man, I gotta give you props for investing in this record store, man. That must've been a lot of money. How much are we really worth? We? [LAUGHING] 'We.'
jokes.setup: Will asks about family wealth, assuming he's included
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil laughs at Will thinking he's part of the inheritance
jokes.explanation: Classic Uncle Phil dismissiveness toward Will's presumptions. The repeated 'We' with laughter sells Phil's amusement at Will's audacity.

jokes.text: It's your opening, I want to look beautiful. Well, Vivian, I don't have that kind of time.
jokes.setup: Vivian explains she wants to look beautiful for Phil's store opening
jokes.punchline: Phil says he doesn't have that much time
jokes.explanation: Standard married couple humor - Phil's impatience masquerading as a backhanded insult. Predictable but delivered well.

jokes.text: Am I antagonizing like Geraldo? Am I investigative like Donahue? Maybe you should go bald like Montel.
jokes.setup: Hilary asks about her talk show persona, comparing herself to TV hosts
jokes.punchline: Carlton suggests she emulate Montel's baldness
jokes.explanation: Carlton's literal-minded response to Hilary's personality questions - missing the point and focusing on Montel's physical trait.

jokes.text: Just G, but we don't take him nowhere anyway.
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil asks if they're missing anyone
jokes.punchline: Will mentions Geoffrey but dismisses him casually
jokes.explanation: Casual dismissal of Geoffrey as unimportant family member. Shows Will's thoughtless attitude toward the help.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil chuckles when Will suggests managing the record store
jokes.setup: Will suggests he could manage the record store
jokes.punchline: Uncle Phil's dismissive chuckle
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat showing Uncle Phil's lack of faith in Will's business acumen. Sets up future conflict.

jokes.text: Will suggests adding 'X-rated skin flicks' to make money 'hand over fist'
jokes.setup: Will offers business advice for the record store
jokes.punchline: Immediately suggests pornography as a business strategy
jokes.explanation: Typical Will inappropriate suggestion that shows his street-smart but unsophisticated business thinking. Mildly funny but relies on shock value.

jokes.text: [YELLING] I mean, Mother, you watch my show, am I too soft-spoken?
jokes.setup: Hilary worries about being too soft-spoken on TV
jokes.punchline: Shouts the question, demonstrating the opposite problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic character moment - Hilary's complete lack of self-awareness shown through contradictory behavior. Well-structured physical comedy.

jokes.text: Why, son, when I was your age and I was working on that farm... boy, I had to work from 9 in the night to 9 forever. I just worked and...
jokes.setup: Phil starts typical 'when I was your age' lecture about hard work
jokes.punchline: Gets confused in his own story, nonsensically saying '9 forever'
jokes.explanation: Phil's rambling old-timer story breaks down into nonsense. Shows how his lectures can become incoherent when he gets carried away.

jokes.text: Ashley's song interrupts Carlton's rambling story
jokes.setup: Carlton is mid-boring story
jokes.punchline: Gets completely cut off by Ashley's music
jokes.explanation: Good timing joke that saves the audience from Carlton's rambling. Simple but effective scene transition comedy.

jokes.text: Will's 'Ching-ching!' reaction to hearing Ashley sing
jokes.setup: Ashley demonstrates her singing talent
jokes.punchline: Will immediately sees dollar signs
jokes.explanation: Perfect Will moment - immediately seeing the business opportunity. Good character integration and economy.

jokes.text: You'd be surprised what a little creativity in the studio can do. Ask Janet Jackson.
jokes.setup: Ashley praises how professional her demo sounds
jokes.punchline: Will references Janet Jackson's studio enhancement/lip-syncing controversy
jokes.explanation: Topical reference to music industry studio manipulation. Gets a knowing laugh from audiences aware of the Janet Jackson controversies.

jokes.text: Well, you're certainly losing that battle.
jokes.setup: Phil says 'I don't want any buts'
jokes.punchline: Carlton observes Phil is losing the battle against having a big butt
jokes.explanation: Carlton's innocent fat joke about Uncle Phil. Well-timed and uses the word play on 'buts/butts' but relies on physical humor.

jokes.text: Now, we better start our walk, Vivian. Baskin-Robbins closes at 6.
jokes.setup: Phil suggests they go for a walk for exercise
jokes.punchline: Reveals the walk is actually to get ice cream
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phil character moment - his 'exercise' is really about food. The Baskin-Robbins detail makes it specific and believable.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil says they need to walk to Baskin-Robbins because it 'closes at 6'
jokes.setup: Uncle Phil suggests they go for a walk
jokes.punchline: Reveals the walk is really about ice cream timing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Uncle Phil's priorities and love of food. The reveal that the 'walk' is ice cream-motivated is perfectly Phil.

jokes.text: We ask his permission, he says no, we do it anyway.
jokes.setup: Ashley worried about Phil saying no to her singing career
jokes.punchline: Will calmly outlines his plan to ignore Phil's authority
jokes.explanation: Will's matter-of-fact approach to rebellion. His casual tone makes the insubordination funnier than if he were angry or scheming.

jokes.text: You know me, the pool party at Q's crib. I'm sorry. Q doesn't have a pool.
jokes.setup: Will tries to charm receptionist with fake shared memory
jokes.punchline: She immediately disproves his story with specific knowledge
jokes.explanation: Will's hustle attempt immediately backfires. Good setup/payoff structure showing his schemes don't always work.

jokes.text: You feisty, huh? What, are you Creole? That's it.
jokes.setup: Receptionist threatens to call cops on Will
jokes.punchline: Will's inappropriate flirting escalates to ethnic stereotyping, causing her to follow through
jokes.explanation: Will's continued inappropriate behavior leading to consequences. The Creole comment feels dated and relies on stereotypes.

jokes.text: Speaker for Mr. Berry. We didn't order any speakers. It's a gift from Sony Music... from Mr. Sock-It-To-Me.
jokes.setup: Will poses as delivery man to gain entry
jokes.punchline: Makes up ridiculous executive name 'Mr. Sock-It-To-Me'
jokes.explanation: Will's improvised con with silly fake name. Shows his quick thinking and charm, though the name is more absurd than clever.

jokes.text: Will pretends to deliver speakers from 'Mr. Sock-It-To-Me' at Sony Music
jokes.setup: Will needs to get into the building
jokes.punchline: Makes up the ridiculous name 'Mr. Sock-It-To-Me'
jokes.explanation: Will's quick thinking and street hustle on display. The fake name is absurd enough to be funny while showing his scheming nature.

jokes.text: Hey, listen, I am the one that is responsible... for getting Ashford and Simpson back together. I deserve a damn Image Award. Quincy? Oh, come on, man, he gets one every year. The cheapskate, he won't even buy a pool.
jokes.setup: Will fake-brags on phone about music industry achievements
jokes.punchline: Calls Quincy Jones cheap and references the pool callback
jokes.explanation: Will's continued con work with callback to earlier pool joke. Shows his commitment to the bit and ability to build on established lies.

jokes.text: Will says Quincy Jones won't buy a pool because he's a 'cheapskate'
jokes.setup: Earlier joke about Q not having a pool
jokes.punchline: Will calls the famous producer cheap
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the earlier pool joke, maintaining the running gag while showing Will's irreverent attitude toward celebrities.

jokes.text: [Long phone conversation with lots of 'uh-huhs' and agreements while Will sneaks around]
jokes.setup: Gordy Berry having serious phone conversation
jokes.punchline: Visual of Will sneaking around office while Gordy unknowingly agrees
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy sequence of Will infiltrating office while Berry is distracted. Visual gag relies on timing and audience seeing both activities.

jokes.text: Hey, did you know James Brown got an orthopedic cape?
jokes.setup: Will trying to impress Berry with music knowledge
jokes.punchline: Offers absurd fake fact about James Brown's cape
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd fake trivia combining James Brown's famous cape with medical equipment. Perfectly random and quotable.

jokes.text: But you know what? You better get a good look at this face and remember it... because when Ashley Banks' album goes double platinum... you're gonna be seeing it in your dreams and in your nightmares... because you ain't the only game in town.
jokes.setup: Gordy Berry rejects Ashley's demo
jokes.punchline: Will launches into over-the-top threat/prediction speech
jokes.explanation: Will's dramatic response to rejection shows his loyalty and delusion. The 'dreams and nightmares' line is memorable melodrama.

jokes.text: Oh, and p.s... the Jheri curl played out with the jerk.
jokes.setup: Will finishing his dramatic exit speech
jokes.punchline: Adds fashion criticism about Berry's outdated hairstyle
jokes.explanation: Perfect parting shot - Will gets personal with dated cultural reference. The 'p.s.' format makes it feel like an afterthought burn.

jokes.text: This is the music business. You need the universal lubricant. I'm talking cash, man.
jokes.setup: DJ asks how Will expects him to help with no payment
jokes.punchline: Calls money 'universal lubricant' with suggestive phrasing
jokes.explanation: DJ's crude but effective metaphor for bribery. The innuendo is obvious but fits the sleazy radio character.

jokes.text: Brother, I was just cleaning the executive suite up on the 24th floor. They're talking about getting rid of you. Yeah, say you're slipping. One of them said your mom have a limp.
jokes.setup: Will pretends to have overheard executives discussing firing the DJ
jokes.punchline: Escalates to absurd personal insult about DJ's mother's limp
jokes.explanation: Will's manipulation tactics include bizarre personal details. The limp comment is so random it becomes funny through absurdity.

jokes.text: Will does multiple fake caller voices requesting Ashley's song, including threats to 'blow up your car'
jokes.setup: Will needs to create fake demand for Ashley's song
jokes.punchline: The increasingly extreme fake callers, ending with a bomb threat
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Will's scheme with varied voices. The bomb threat escalates it to absurdity while showing Will's desperation.

jokes.text: [IN HIGH VOICE] How are you doing? This is Rafica calling from Watts. Ho! Could you play that record by Ashley Banks called 'Make Up Your Mind'? I'm with this man who can't make up his mind.
jokes.setup: Will takes over DJ booth to fake radio requests
jokes.punchline: Does exaggerated female voice with story matching the song
jokes.explanation: Will's voice impersonation to create fake demand. The matching personal story to song title shows clever preparation.

jokes.text: [IN FOREIGN ACCENT] My name is Tony. And I'd like to hear that record by Ashley Banks called 'Make Up Your Mind'... before I have to come down there and blow up your car.
jokes.setup: Will doing another fake caller voice
jokes.punchline: Escalates to violent threat in foreign accent
jokes.explanation: Will's second fake caller with more extreme threat. Shows escalation in his fake demand campaign but relies on stereotype accent.

jokes.text: Will tricks someone by saying 'There's nobody up there' then 'Made you look'
jokes.setup: Someone is looking for executives upstairs
jokes.punchline: Classic playground trick applied to adult situation
jokes.explanation: Simple playground humor that shows Will's immature side. Gets a mild laugh from the childishness in an adult setting.

jokes.text: Get it? Feather, peacock. [CARLTON LAUGHS]
jokes.setup: Carlton introduces Ashley as 'feather in our peacock cap' at Peacock Club
jokes.punchline: Explains his own pun and laughs at it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton moment - making obvious pun then explaining it to make sure everyone gets it. His laugh at his own joke is very characteristic.

jokes.text: Damn, she must have stage fright. Carlton, help me. Sorry, folks, wawhhh, a little technical difficulty.
jokes.setup: Ashley freezes on stage during her big performance
jokes.punchline: Will and Carlton scramble with awkward technical difficulty excuse
jokes.explanation: Cringe moment as Will's big plan falls apart. The awkward technical difficulty cover-up adds to the embarrassment.

jokes.text: He got a wooden plate, found out he got termites.
jokes.setup: Will trying to excuse Uncle Phil's bad mood
jokes.punchline: Offers bizarre explanation about wooden plate and termites
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly random Will explanation that makes no logical sense. The specificity of 'wooden plate' and 'termites' makes it memorably absurd.

jokes.text: That ain't help nothing, did it?
jokes.setup: Will tells Berry he'll be managing Ashley to reassure Phil
jokes.punchline: Realizes his management credentials don't reassure anyone
jokes.explanation: Perfect self-aware moment where Will realizes his credibility is the problem, not the solution. Great timing and delivery.

jokes.text: Don't you give me that look. Vivian, she's giving me that look.
jokes.setup: Ashley presumably gives Phil puppy dog eyes about the record deal
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately appeals to Vivian for backup against the look
jokes.explanation: Classic father-daughter dynamic with Phil's vulnerability to Ashley's manipulation. His panic at 'the look' is relatable.

jokes.text: Shadow? Uncle Phil, with you, it'd be more like an eclipse, wouldn't it? [BOTH LAUGHING] Eclipse. Eclipse.
jokes.setup: Phil says he'll be on Will like a shadow
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Phil's size makes it more like an eclipse
jokes.explanation: Excellent fat joke with clever astronomical upgrade from shadow to eclipse. Phil and Carlton's laughter shows it landed perfectly.

jokes.text: You know, just for that crack, I'm gonna make myself co-manager. One fat joke too many. I knew it, I could feel it.
jokes.setup: Will makes the eclipse fat joke
jokes.punchline: Phil declares consequences while acknowledging his own weight sensitivity
jokes.explanation: Phil's self-aware response to being the target of fat jokes. 'I could feel it' is perfect Phil - dramatic but self-knowing.

tmdb_overview: Will revels in Ashley's music industry success, but both Will and Ashley are about to have a rude surprise.
